records.specific_details: The Wheel hosted launch event for our Pre-Budget Submission 2026. It comprised a panel discussion with speakers from community and voluntary organisations and an overview of The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026. We invited Ministers, TDs and Senators from the most relevant departments and joint Oireachtas committees and several attended in person.
records.intended_results: To inform TDs and Senators about The Wheel's priorities for Budget 2026, which includes issues such as sustainable funding for the sector, equality of pay, increased compliance requirements, and the hidden infrastructure provided by the sector in areas such as health, social care, housing and climate action.

records.specific_details: Emails to Members of the Oireachtas Committee on Social Protection, Community & Local Development requesting to put combatting poverty at the top of the committees agenda, with particular reference to income adequacy, accessible public services & measures targeted at the most disadvantaged groups
records.intended_results: To influence decision makers to be more conscious of the impact of poverty on the individuals affected and society as a whole by providing information and research in relation to legislation and policy in this area with particular regard to the area of income adequacy, availability of public services, and targeted measures for the most disadvantaged gorups
lobbying_activities: Email contact with Chair & Members of the Committee for Social Protection, Community & Rural Development requesting contact in relation to the work of the committee in pre-budget preparation, the need for income adequacy, the new Anti-Poverty strategy and the need to add socio-economic disadvantage as a ground for discrimination in Equality legislation - Email

Email request to Liam Quaide TD & Mark Wall TD to submit Parliamentary Questions in relation to income adequacy, the need for socio-economic status to be included as a ground for discrimination under the equality act. - Email

Follow on meeting with Liam Quaide TD and the assistant of Mark Wall TD in Leinster House 17/6 in relation to their work as Social Protection Spokespersons for their parties and the work of the committee - Meeting

records.specific_details: a number of questions pertaining to older persons care in congregated settings and in the community
records.intended_results: Answers to the concerns among the IASW members that social work services are missing and promised posts remain vacant. There are further concerns that allegations of abuse or harm may not be getting passed on from one agency to the HSE Safeguarding teams in order to adequately reflect the volume of allegations or level of harm being experienced in both hospital/ nursing home and community settings.
lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: Sent Pre-Budget Submission to the Joint Oireachtas Committee on Social Protection. This Submission was invited by the Committee as part of the annual Pre-Budget process. We have registered this lobbying with each member of the Committee out of an abundance of caution.
records.intended_results: i) Publish a timeline for implementation of the Programme for Government Actionto abolish the means test for Carers Allowance within the lifetime of thisGovernment.  ::i) Increase the weekly limit for education, work and voluntary hours for those inreceipt of Carers Allowance, Carers Benefit, and Carer Support Grant. Werecommend a minimum of 20 hours per week.ii) Examine the current methods for calculation of the permissible working hoursto take account of once-off, seasonal work available to family carers. ::i) Raise the levels of payments for Carers Allowanceii) Support the recent ask from the Oireachtas Disability Group, calling for a Costof Disability payment of €55 per week (€2, 860 per annum)iii) Raise the level of the Carer Support Grant to €2,060, in line with the projectedaverage wage increase in 2026.
lobbying_activities: Email

records.specific_details: Email concerning the E-liquids Products Tax and concerns around the mechanism chosen to implement the tax via self-declaration rather than as a tax-stamp. Hale also referred to research commissioned from KPMG which raised several important policy considerations around the E-Liquids products tax including the potential for a surge in illicit and grey market sales.
records.intended_results: A review of the proposal to apply an E-liquids tax using self-declaration mechanism rather than a tax stamp.

records.specific_details: Support for Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug
records.intended_results: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. As such, Hale have never stocked HHC products, which Hale view as unregulated and potentially harmful substance.

dpo_entries.public_body: Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas
Dáil Éireann, the Lower House of the Oireachtas

lobbying_activities: Offer full support from Hale Vaping, Ireland’s largest indigenous vaping company for the proposed Classification of HHC as a Schedule 1 Controlled Drug by the Government. Hale expressed strong support for the proposed legislative action to classify hexahydrocannabinol (HHC) as a Schedule 1 controlled drug under the Misuse of Drugs Act 1977. Hale Vaping is not affiliated with the tobacco industry and has always upheld high standards of compliance and public health responsibility. - Email

records.specific_details: We contacted Deputies Quaide, Cummins, O'Callaghan, and Rice in their respective capacities as the Social Democrats' disability,  finance, social protection, education, and health spokespeople, to invite them to a briefing of Inclusion Ireland's Pre-Budget Submission's priorities, along with a fully copy of the document for their consideration.
records.intended_results: Inclusion Ireland sought to influence the Social Democrats' proposals around disability matters ahead of their own Pre-Budget Submission.
lobbying_activities: Deputies Rice, Quaide, and O'Callaghan directly replied to our overtures to confirm their receiving of our proposals and that they would pass them on to the Social Democrats' Policy Director. - Email

records.specific_details: Inclusion Ireland, along with colleagues from other organisations making up the Oireachtas Disability Group, met with the Social Democrats' Disability spokesperson Liam Quaide TD present our collective priorities ahead of Budget 2026.
records.intended_results: As members of the Oireachtas Disability Group, Inclusion Ireland sought to influence the Social Democrats' proposals around disability matters ahead of their own Pre-Budget Submission.
lobbying_activities: Members of the Oireachtas Disability Group met with Deputy Quaide over Zoom, along with the Deputy's parliamentary assistant and the Social Democrats' Policy Director. - Virtual Meeting

records.specific_details: Circulation of PSI Press Release to Oireachtas Members (opposition TDs) Calling for an End to the Use of Therapy Notes in Rape and Sexual Assault Cases
records.intended_results: The PSI seeks to secure legislative reform prohibiting the use of therapy notes in legal proceedings involving sexual assault, to protect survivor confidentiality and access to psychological care. PSI aims to establish its expertise as the professional voice for psychology on this issue, influence opposition TDs to support relevant legislation, and prompt Government action on trauma-informed, rights-based policies in response to gender-based violence.
lobbying_activities: PSI circulated a press release calling for an end to the use of therapy notes in sexual assault legal proceedings. The release set out PSI’s rationale against the practice, referenced PSI’s Position Statement, CSO data on sexual violence, ethical guidelines, and survivor concerns. The activity aimed to inform designated public officials, support survivor-centred legislation, and position PSI as a stakeholder in shaping reform. - Email

records.specific_details: Meeting with Deputies Liam Quaide and Rory Hearne on the Mental Health Bill 2024, Proposed Amendments, and Assessment of Need Policy
records.intended_results: The discussion focused on the PSI’s position on the Bill, proposed amendments, and concerns regarding the current implementation of the Assessment of Need, including improving access, timeliness, and equity in mental health services.	Engagement with Oireachtas members to ensure awareness of PSI’s position on the Mental Health Bill 2024 and the Assessment of Need policy, and to facilitate consideration of PSI’s evidence-informed recommendations during legislative and policy development.
lobbying_activities: Meeting with Deputies Liam Quaide and Rory Hearne to present the PSI’s position on the proposed Mental Health Bill 2024, discuss amendments, and address concerns about the Assessment of Need policy, including proposals to improve access and service delivery. - Meeting

records.specific_details: Joint letter from the PSI and allied professional bodies to TDs outlining concerns re the HSE’s draft Autism Assessment & Intervention Protocol, including Tier 1 assessments, alignment with best practice, and calling for urgent engagement with the HSE.
records.intended_results: PSI and other professional bodies circulated a joint letter originally sent to John Fitzmaurice of the HSE to Oireachtas members to raise concerns re the HSE’s draft Autism Assessment & Intervention Protocol. The activity aimed to ensure elected representatives were informed of professional positions, highlight issues around Tier 1 assessments and adherence to guidelines, and encourage engagement with the HSE to support safe, evidence-based, and effective implementation in the public interest.
lobbying_activities: Circulation of the joint letter from Odhrán Allen (AOTI), signed by multiple professional bodies including PSI, to TDs Liam Quaide and Eoin O’Broin regarding the Autism Assessment & Intervention Protocol and requesting engagement with the HSE. - Informal communication
